DISCOVERY, 302, PLAINTIFF MARC BRUNO'S MOTION TO COMPEL SPRIG INC.S COMPLIANCE TO PLAINTIFFS DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR THE PRODUCTOIN OF BUSINESS RECORDS, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS TO THE SUBSTATIVE PART OF THE MOTION TO COMPEL AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO SANCTIONS. THIRD-PARTY SPRING, INC. IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE THE DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THE SUBPOENA, LIMITED PER THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES ((MPA, 2:15-18) WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER BY OVERNIGHT DELIVERY. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF THIS DATE BY JUDGE PRO TEM PETER CATALANOTTI. SPRIG INC. DID NOT "UNSUCCESSFULLY...OPPOSE A MOTION TO COMPEL." CCP SECTION 2025.480(J). THE MOVING PARTY SUBMITS A PROPOSED ORDER. THE COURT MAY SIGN THE SUBMITTED PROPOSED ORDER OR ISSUE ITS OWN. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (DISC302)

DISCOVERY, 302, PLAINTIFF MARC BRUNO'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT SAMUEL BATYS FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION IN PART. THE MOTION IS TENTATIVELY DENIED AS MOOT AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 15. THE MOTION IS TENTATIVELY GRANTED AS TO REQUEST NO. 11. WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THE COURT'S ORDER, DEFENDANT SHALL EITHER PRODUCE HIS CELL PHONE RECORDS TO PLAINTIFF, OR PROVIDE AUTHORIZATION TO HIS CELL PHONE SERVICE PROVIDER TO SEND THE RECORDS DIRECTLY TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. SANCTIONS ARE UNDER SUBMISSION. THE COURT SHALL ISSUE ITS OWN WRITTEN ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: NAOMI JANE GRAY; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (DISC302)

CORRECTED DECLARATION OF NICHOLAS A. CARLIN IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO ATTEND AND TESTIFY AT DEPOSITION AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 61299258) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BRUNO, MARC

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS SAMUEL BATY, DINA SMITH'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO ATTEND AND TESTIFY AT DEPOSITION AND AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFF IS TO SET A DATE CERTAIN FOR HIS DEPOSITION IN JANUARY OF 2018. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER TO PROVIDE FOUR POTENTIAL DATES FOR HIS DEPOSITION IN JANUARY OF 2018. DEFENDANT SHALL SET AND NOTICE PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION FOR ONE OF THESE FOUR DATES. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR THE DEPOSITION ON THE NOTICED DATE CHOSEN. SHOULD NONE OF THE FOUR DATES WORK FOR DEFENDANTS, COUNSELS SHALL WORK TOGETHER TO FIND A MUTUALLY CONVENIENT DATE IN JANUARY OF 2018 FOR THE DEPOSITION OF THE PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 TO THE COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: ELIZABETH RILES, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D302/JPT/HK)

MOTION To Compel Plaintiff To Attend And Testify At Deposition And Award Of Monetary Sanctions Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Thomas J. Feeney In Support
Off Calendar Nov-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted.

DEFENDANTS SAMUEL BATY, DINA SMITH's MOTION To Compel Plaintiff To Attend And Testify At Deposition And Award Of Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant's motion is granted in part. Plaintiff is to set a date certain for his deposition in January of 2018. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the date of this order to provide four potential dates for his deposition in January of 2018. Defendant shall set and notice Plaintiff's deposition for one of these four dates. Plaintiff is ordered to appear for the deposition on the noticed date chosen. Should none of the four dates work for Defendants, counsels shall work together to find a mutually convenient date in January of 2018 for the deposition of the Plaintiff. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 to the Court.

Judge Pro Tem: Elizabeth Riles, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/JPT/HK)

MOTION To Compel Defendant Dina Smiths Further Responses, Etc.
Off Calendar Nov-21-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Anna Gourgiotopoulou of the law offices of Phillips Erlewine Given & Carlin LLP (415-413-4684) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Thomas J. Feeney of the law offices of Carbone Smoke Smith Bent & Leonard (510-267-7273) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Stipulation and order appointing judge pro tem is signed and filed.

The cause is submitted without argument as to the motion and argued and submitted by both sides as to sanctions.

PLAINTIFF MARC BRUNO'S MOTION To Compel Defendant Dina Smiths Further Responses, Etc. is
GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART and TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION IN PART. The motion is tentatively denied as moot as to request for production no. 15. The motion is tentatively granted as to request no. 11. Within 10 days of entry of the Court's order, Defendant shall either produce his cell phone records to Plaintiff, or provide authorization to his cell phone service provider to send the records directly to Plaintiff's counsel. Sanctions are UNDER SUBMISSION. The Court shall issue its own written order.

Judge Pro Tem: Naomi Jane Gray; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (DISC302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Sprig Inc.S Compliance To Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For The Productoin Of Business Records, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Anna Gourgiotopoulou of the law offices of Phillips Erlewine Given & Carlin LLP (415-398-0900) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

Stipulation and order appointing judge pro tem is signed and filed.

The cause is submitted on the tentative ruling as to the motion to compel and argued and submitted by the moving party as to sanctions.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling as to the motion only.

PLAINTIFF MARC BRUNO'S Motion To Compel Sprig Inc.S Compliance To Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For The Productoin Of Business Records, And For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED as to the substative part of the motion to compel and taken UNDER SUBMISSION as to sanctions. Third-Party Spring, Inc. is ordered to provide the documents responsive to the subpoena, limited per the agreement of the parties ((MPA, 2:15-18) within two weeks of notice of this order by overnight delivery. The request for sanctions is taken under submission as of this date by Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti. Sprig Inc. did not "unsuccessfully...oppose a motion to compel." CCP Section 2025.480(j). The moving party submits a proposed order. The Court may sign the submitted proposed order or issue its own.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter catalanotti: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (DISC302)
